cards.back: An array that can store elements of any data type in rows and columns.

cards.front: What is used to declare a two-dimensional array in C++?
What is the difference between a one-dimensional and two-dimensional array?
cards.back: A two-dimensional array can store elements in rows and columns, while a one-dimensional array stores elements in a single sequence.

cards.front: How is a two-dimensional array initialized?
cards.back: It can be initialized using the syntax: {rowsNumber}[columnsNumber][rowsNumber]; or using the syntax: new type[rowsNumber][columnsNumber];

cards.front: What is a matrix in the context of data structures?
cards.back: A matrix is a two-dimensional array that represents a rectangular region of rows and columns, often used to store and manipulate data.
